title: Trade Profit
query: SELECT COALESCE(SUM(TotalSale), 0) AS total, COALESCE((SELECT SUM(TotalCost) FROM MarketBuy WHERE event_timestamp >= :startDate AND event_timestamp < :endDate), 0) AS spent, COUNT(*) AS sales FROM MarketSell WHERE event_timestamp >= :startDate AND event_timestamp < :endDate

details.text: {sales} market sale(s) generating {total} Cr in revenue during the report period, with {spent} Cr spent buying goods. Net profit represents your actual earnings after the cost of cargo — the trader's true scorecard.
details.help: total (sale revenue) drives the title. spent comes from a subquery against MarketBuy. Net = total - spent. Both tables needed for profit calculation.

tagLines: Trade ledger reconciled. Revenue and cost of goods are logged. The margin is yours to interpret.
Buy low, sell high. Simple in theory. The market has opinions about when you try it.
Every credit of profit is a route that worked, a price that held, and a cargo hold that wasn't wasted.
